title: Net Metering Policy by State (2026)
note: How each state compensates rooftop-solar exports at the statewide/dominant-utility level: full retail 1:1 credit, net billing at a reduced export rate, or no statewide mandate. Net metering is often set utility-by-utility, so your specific credit can differ; confirm with your utility and the DSIRE database. Most states grandfather existing customers 10-20 years when rules change.

rows.detail: No statewide net metering; Alabama Power adds a solar fee and pays avoided cost.
Applies to Railbelt utilities only, up to 25 kW.
Retail net metering ended 2017; export ~7-10 c/kWh, set per utility.
1:1 retained for smaller systems under recent PSC grandfathering.
NEM 3.0 export credit ~5-8 c/kWh (down from ~30 c); NEM 2.0 grandfathered 9 yrs.
Statewide 1:1 retail-rate credit for residential exports, typically with monthly rollover.
Statewide 1:1 retail-rate credit for residential exports, typically with monthly rollover.
Statewide 1:1 retail-rate credit for residential exports, typically with monthly rollover.
Statewide 1:1 retail-rate credit for residential exports, typically with monthly rollover.
1:1 preserved; a 2022 rollback was vetoed.
Georgia Power nets instantaneously at below-retail; 1:1 program capped and full.
Traditional NEM closed; newer tariffs (e.g. grid-supply/CGS) pay well below retail.
No statewide mandate; Idaho Power uses a reduced export credit study.
Statewide 1:1 retail-rate credit for residential exports, typically with monthly rollover.
Moved to wholesale-plus export (2022); existing NEM grandfathered to 2032.
Statewide 1:1 retail-rate credit for residential exports, typically with monthly rollover.
Statewide 1:1 retail-rate credit for residential exports, typically with monthly rollover.
2021 law let utilities file lower export rates; PSC approved reduced credits.
Retail net metering ended for new systems (2020); credits at avoided cost.
Statewide 1:1 retail-rate credit for residential exports, typically with monthly rollover.
Statewide 1:1 retail-rate credit for residential exports, typically with monthly rollover.
Full retail with monthly rollover and annual cash-out.
Inflow/outflow 'distributed generation' tariff credits exports below retail.
Statewide 1:1 retail-rate credit for residential exports, typically with monthly rollover.
Net billing with a small low-income adder; below retail.
Statewide 1:1 retail-rate credit for residential exports, typically with monthly rollover.
Statewide 1:1 retail-rate credit for residential exports, typically with monthly rollover.
Statewide 1:1 retail-rate credit for residential exports, typically with monthly rollover.
Tiered export credit (~75% of retail and declining) under NV Energy.
Statewide 1:1 retail-rate credit for residential exports, typically with monthly rollover.
Full retail, no statewide residential cap, 12-month rollover.
Statewide 1:1 retail-rate credit for residential exports, typically with monthly rollover.
1:1 up to 25 kW; larger systems use the VDER value stack.
Duke 'bridge'/net billing with time-of-use and monthly fees since 2023.
Net metering required only up to avoided-cost credit, below retail.
Credits the generation portion of the bill, not the full retail rate.
Weak statewide rule; utilities may meter exports separately at avoided cost.
Statewide 1:1 retail-rate credit for residential exports, typically with monthly rollover.
Full retail today, though PPL has proposed cutting export credits.
Net metering scaled back in 2023 toward a renewable-energy-growth tariff.
Solar Choice net billing after Act 62; time-of-use export values.
No statewide net metering mandate; terms set by each utility.
TVA territory has no retail net metering; exports bought at low generation rates.
Deregulated market; no statewide rule, but many retailers offer 'solar buyback' plans.
Rocky Mountain Power export credit ~5-6 c/kWh, far below retail.
Net metering credit intact but the adjuster has declined several years running.
Statewide 1:1 retail-rate credit for residential exports, typically with monthly rollover.
Statewide 1:1 retail-rate credit for residential exports, typically with monthly rollover.
Statewide 1:1 retail-rate credit for residential exports, typically with monthly rollover.
Statewide 1:1 retail-rate credit for residential exports, typically with monthly rollover.
1:1 for systems up to 25 kW.
